title: PlanetScale OAuth tokens should not be disclosed
description: In most cases, trust boundaries are violated when a secret is exposed in a source code repository or an uncontrolled deployment environment. Unintended people who don’t need to know the secret might get access to it. They might then be able to use it to gain unwanted access to associated services or resources.
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

checks.message: PlanetScale OAuth token detected - CRITICAL. PlanetScale OAuth service token exposed. These credentials provide API access to PlanetScale databases and management operations with full privileges. OAuth tokens can read schemas, execute queries, manage branches, and access sensitive database metadata. IMMEDIATE ACTIONS: 1) Remove token from source code, 2) Revoke via PlanetScale dashboard, 3) Generate new token, 4) Use environment variables or secrets managers (AWS/Vault/Azure), 5) Audit API access logs for unauthorized usage.

PlanetScale service token detected - CRITICAL. PlanetScale API service token exposed. These tokens provide programmatic access to PlanetScale API for database operations, schema management, and administrative functions. IMMEDIATE ACTIONS: 1) Remove token from source code, 2) Revoke via PlanetScale Service Tokens dashboard, 3) Generate replacement token with minimal required scopes, 4) Store in environment variables or secrets managers, 5) Review access logs for suspicious activity.
